name: Jimmy Madison
abstract: James Henry Madison (born February 17, 1947, Cincinnati) is an American jazz drummer with a career spanning over six decades. He started playing the drums professionally at age 10 and was considered a child prodigy.

works.t: Suite for Cello and Jazz Piano Trio No. 1: Ballade
Suite for Cello and Jazz Piano Trio No. 1: Baroque-in-Rhythm

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Madison played drums and Jay Leonhart played bass. It was a really good band that he called the New York All-Star Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: But we had this very freestyle drummer too, Jimmy Madison. He was on most the tracks
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: So there was a bunch of my contemporaries, Jimmy Madison, a great drummer in that time, so always trying to have little gigs of our own
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember hearing Bob with Chet, Harold Danko, Michael Moore, and Jimmy Madison.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
